picks.name: Booking Holdings Inc.
picks.thesis: BKNG is down 25% from its 52-week high while earnings grew 21%/yr — a sentiment-driven pullback, not fundamental deterioration. Quality looks intact (healthy margins, durable revenue, long-term uptrend), and it's now at or below fair value ($174.62 vs $186.00, medium confidence). Valued with DCF and Comps.

methods.name: DDM — dividend discount
methods.reason: Not meaningful: this is a growth-oriented company that reinvests and returns cash mainly via buybacks rather than dividends (yield 0.9%, payout 23%), so a dividend-only model understates it — DDM excluded

reconciliation.sentence: The methods land between $161.36 and $210.64 (a 31% spread). They broadly agree on direction but differ on magnitude — medium confidence.

capital: Dividends: $1.2B paid in FY2025 — 14% of free cash flow
Buybacks: diluted share count down 19% over 3y
Leverage rising: total debt UP 48% over 3y ($13.0B → $19.3B)
change_mind: Price recovers above fair value (~$186.00) — the discount that makes it a buy is gone
Net margin (20%) deteriorates toward the 5% quality floor
manual: Moat & competitive position — is the advantage durable?
Management quality and incentives (proxy statement, capital-allocation record)
Pending litigation or regulatory action beyond headline news
Upcoming catalysts — product cycles, guidance changes, M&A
why: medium confidence across 2 models — DCF $161.36, Comps $210.64
profile: ✓ → multiple compression: price down 25% while earnings grew +21%/yr (sentiment, not deterioration)
